scope_description: Windows support current head before release
head: 26022c8

reviewer_manifest.execution_note: Six-reviewer full panel for Windows support current head before release; scope d0227bf..26022c8.

findings.title: Existing protected env file without HERDCTL_WEB_SECRET cannot recover
findings.summary: A protected herdctl.env that exists but lacks HERDCTL_WEB_SECRET is validated and read, then the generated secret is written through create_new(true), which fails because the file already exists.
findings.proposed_fix: Split new-file creation from validated-existing-file update; append or atomically rewrite the already protected file while preserving owner-only protection, with a regression test.

findings.title: Windows native-root failures still panic through infallible config APIs
findings.summary: Several Windows config/data/profile helpers unwrap native folder discovery instead of returning actionable startup errors.
findings.proposed_fix: Introduce fallible config/data/profile APIs and thread the errors through startup/config creation.

findings.title: Windows smoke can prove the wrong pane or agent
findings.summary: The smoke waits for any gateway agent and falls back to the first agent when the named smoke agent is not found.
findings.proposed_fix: Generate a unique smoke agent name, require that exact agent to appear, and remove fallback-to-first-agent.

findings.title: Windows help advertises the wrong default config path
findings.summary: The CLI help still hardcodes ~/.config/herdr-go/config.json even though Windows uses roaming AppData.
findings.proposed_fix: Render the platform default config path from config::default_config_path() or use cfg-specific help text.

findings.title: Public specs describe Windows Herdr endpoints under the profile while code uses AppData
findings.summary: Specs say the default/named Herdr endpoints are rooted in the native user profile, but the resolver uses roaming AppData joined with herdr.
findings.proposed_fix: Update specs to say Herdr endpoints are under roaming AppData, while allowed workspace defaults use the native profile.

findings.title: Herdr socket calls can hang indefinitely after connect
findings.summary: SocketHerdr reads until newline/EOF without an end-to-end request timeout, so health/recovery can stall on a silent peer.
findings.proposed_fix: Wrap the complete request exchange in a bounded timeout and test an accepted but silent peer plus supervisor restart behavior.

findings.title: Legacy filesystem migration is not directly tested
findings.summary: Startup calls migration and config contains migrate_one, but tests cover only the CLI mode decision seam, not filesystem move/preserve behavior.
findings.proposed_fix: Add tempdir-backed tests for legacy-only move, canonical-plus-legacy preserve, no-op, and rename-error propagation.

findings.title: Named-pipe busy and late-endpoint timing lack regression coverage
findings.summary: The Windows connect loop retries pipe-busy but the suite does not prove busy-to-success, exhausted busy, or late endpoint behavior.
findings.proposed_fix: Add an injectable connection-attempt seam or a Windows smoke subcase that starts gateway before Herdr and proves bounded recovery.

findings.title: Transport dependency drifted from the original no-new-dependency cell contract
findings.summary: The implementation now intentionally uses interprocess for Windows local sockets, while the original cell contract said no new transport dependency.
findings.proposed_fix: Either replace the adapter with direct Tokio named-pipe code or update the approved plan/cell rationale to make interprocess the explicit compatibility boundary.

findings.title: Installation spec still calls second-user token denial an open proof gap
findings.summary: One spec sentence still says real Windows second-user denial is open even though the current Windows Server 2022 smoke proves it.
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite the stale sentence to distinguish proven Server 2022 token denial from remaining Windows 11/archive/installer gaps.

findings.title: Restart spawn failures are swallowed
findings.summary: Supervisor restart errors are ignored, so a missing HERDCTL_HERDR_BINARY produces weak diagnostics during recovery.
findings.proposed_fix: Log restart errors and distinguish spawn failure from successful restart attempt in the health transition seam.

gate4.reason: Human approved merge after review found 0 P1 findings; P2/P3 findings are recorded as non-blocking backlog.

decision.summary: Windows support current-head review approved for release. Scope d0227bf..26022c8; local verify and CI head passed, including Windows Server 2022 runtime smoke.
